title: 1. Client Overview
content:
- **Business name:** Bobcat Movers
- **Location:** San Marcos, TX 78666 (confirmed from Dialpad 2025). 2nd GMB in Lockhart (tracked Jan 2026 heatmap, may have been dropped by Feb 2026 - GMB footprint changed mid-quarter).
- **Niche:** Moving company / local moving services.
- **Key contacts:**
  - **Blake York** — owner/operator (primary contact 2024 through 2025).
  - **Katie** — confirmed new contact from Nov 2025 onwards, first appeared on a pictures-review call 24/11/25. Role unconfirmed (team member or family member - verify via voice dump).
  - **Address (Dialpad):** 5040 N State Hwy 123, San Marcos TX 78666
  - NOTE: Earlier versions of this file incorrectly listed "Rudy" as owner. That was cross-contamination from Elite Movers notes. Corrected 2026-04-08.
- **Website:** Domain not stated clearly in the preserved notes here; verify before any work starts.
- **Google Business Profile:** GBP/GMB exists, but the direct profile link was not preserved in the available record.
- **Social links:** None clearly mentioned in the available Bobcat project history.
- **Services offered:** Moving services. Based on design/content direction, likely includes residential moving and higher-care / white-glove style moves. Full service list should be verified from the live site.
- **Target areas / locations:** Not explicitly preserved in the available project notes. Must be verified from the site/GBP before delivery work continues.

title: 2. Client Persona & Communication Style
content:
- **Communication style:** Limited direct communication history is preserved in this workspace. The account appears to have been handled more as a straightforward execution client than a highly collaborative one.
- **Likely priorities:** Calls, booked jobs, trust, and premium presentation. Website discussions suggest a strong emphasis on converting visitors quickly and presenting the company as more "elite" / higher-value.
- **Sensitivities / preferences:**
  - Avoid overly wordy website copy above the fold.
  - Strong preference for a call-first conversion path rather than too much informational clutter.
  - Brand direction leaned toward a more premium, careful, higher-end feel rather than generic moving-company language.
- **Preferred update channel:** Not clearly documented in the available Bobcat record.

title: 3. Timeline of Key Events
content:
> Dates below reflect what is visible in the preserved project context. Several earlier actions may have happened but are not clearly timestamped here.

- **Before May 2025**
  - Bobcat Movers was already an active client/project inside Online Optimisers.
  - Website and SEO work had already been underway before the preserved technical issue in May 2025.

- **2025-05-19**
  - A technical access issue was recorded: Bobcat Movers' site was hosted on Bluehost, used WordPress, and showed a **403 error** when trying to access `wp-admin`.
  - Domain registrar was noted as **GoDaddy**.
  - DNS records were pointing to **Bluehost (173.254.29.134)**.
  - Working assumption at the time: the client may have had multiple prior developers, and login/access ownership was unclear.

- **2026-02 to 2026-03 (website/design refinement period)**
  - Website homepage/hero direction was actively discussed.
  - Strategic move: reduce above-the-fold text, remove friction, and push visitors toward calling.
  - Proposed structure included:
    - hero section
    - trust strip
    - CTA block / call-now card
    - stronger premium/white-glove positioning
  - Additional idea introduced: a **white-glove / higher-value homes** section to elevate perceived service quality and allow mention of delicate/antique items.

- **2026-02-03**
  - A link-replacement outreach email was drafted tied to **LocalMovers.com** rebranding.
  - The requested implementation was to replace an existing badge/text with a sentence stating Bobcat Movers had been recognized by LocalMovers.com, using linked anchor text.
  - It is not confirmed in the record whether this link update was actually implemented.

title: 4. SEO / Delivery History
content:
- **Work done / discussed:**
  - Website work and homepage conversion optimization.
  - SEO-oriented website messaging refinement.
  - Link-related work involving a recognition/award-style mention from **LocalMovers.com**.
  - Technical troubleshooting around WordPress/hosting/admin access.

- **What worked well:**
  - Call-first positioning was favored over long copy blocks.
  - A more premium, careful-service angle appears to have fit the brand direction better than generic mover copy.
  - Using Rudy as a visual trust element in hero imagery was discussed as a strength.

- **What did not work / was deprioritized:**
  - Excessive text above the fold was seen as undesirable.
  - Large or overly heavy trust sections were questioned and likely deprioritized in favor of tighter layout.
  - No evidence in the preserved notes of a large-scale content expansion, citation campaign, or project-page rollout for Bobcat specifically.

- **Current state of rankings / traffic / GBP performance:**
  - No hard ranking data, traffic numbers, or GBP metrics were preserved in the available project context.
  - Any current performance assumptions should be treated as unknown until re-baselined.

- **Tools / platforms mentioned:**
  - **Duda CMS** (NOT WordPress - this affects schema implementation options, Duda has schema limitations that may explain 0/25 schema score in Phase 1 audit)
  - Bluehost (hosting, old dev admin user `agreco@mainstreethost.com` - flag for password manager migration)
  - GoDaddy (domain registrar)
  - Google Business Profile / GMB (Main + Lockhart 2nd GMB)
  - LocalMovers.com (link/brand mention context)
  - Chariot CRM (moving-industry specific CRM)
  - AI voice agent in CRM (full lead handling)
  - Templated SMS response system (no full automation - sends webform link after hours)


### Lead volume trend (from Obsidian notes)

| Month | Leads |
|---|---|
| April 2025 | 66 |
| May 2025 | 72 |
| June 2025 | 79 |
| August 2025 | 110 |
| September 2025 | 42 |
| October 2025 | 43 |

**FLAG:** Major drop Sep/Oct 2025 unexplained. Possibly correlated with GMB footprint change (Lockhart dropped, New Braunfels/Canyon Lake added in Feb 2026 heatmaps). Worth asking Blake what changed.


### Recent wins
- Recently surpassed "Little Guys Movers" on review count
- Main gap: new keyword opportunity search from start-date baseline to identify competitor movements


### Additional context
- San Marcos Legacy Business Program 2025 (relevant local angle)
- Form fill outs are main lead source, mainly from Google search

title: 5. Writing Style & Tone Used
content:
- **Tone direction used:**
  - Direct
  - Trust-building
  - Call-focused
  - Premium / careful / high-service
- **Examples of positioning direction:**
  - Reduce text density and drive users to call quickly.
  - Emphasize care for delicate items and nicer homes.
  - Create a more "elite" feel rather than sounding like a low-cost generic mover.
- **Phrases / angles that appeared aligned:**
  - white-glove
  - higher-value homes
  - delicate items
  - extra care
  - premium / elite feel
- **Rejected or questioned messaging approaches:**
  - Too much text above the fold.
  - Oversized trust strip if it felt tacky or distracted from conversion.

title: 6. Ideas Generated (Used or Unused)
content:
- **Used or seriously considered:**
  - Tight, call-first homepage hero.
  - Trust strip under hero copy.
  - CTA card on the right side instead of a lead form.
  - New section focused on white-glove / higher-value homes.
  - Visual emphasis on Rudy in hero/background imagery.
  - Recognition/authority mention via LocalMovers.com.

- **Discussed but not confirmed as actioned:**
  - Adding stronger messaging around antiques/delicate items.
  - Restructuring section order so the site had more explanatory content before the contact form.
  - Balancing premium branding with SEO copy needs.

title: 7. Results & Wins
content:
- **Documented results:**
  - No specific ranking lifts, GBP call gains, traffic numbers, or testimonial quotes were preserved in the available Bobcat project history.
- **Clear qualitative win:**
  - The website direction became clearer: less clutter, more direct-response, more premium service positioning.

title: 8. Open Issues / Unfinished Business
content:
- Confirm exact **website domain**, **GBP link**, **service area**, and **main point of contact**.
- Reconfirm **WordPress / hosting / registrar access** and whether the prior `wp-admin` 403 issue is fully resolved.
- Verify whether the **LocalMovers.com** link change was implemented.
- Pull a fresh baseline for:
  - rankings
  - GBP metrics
  - traffic
  - homepage conversion path
- Confirm final homepage structure and whether the **white-glove / higher-value homes** section was built.
- Verify the approved service list and whether premium-service messaging matches real operational capability.

title: 9. Anything Else Worth Knowing
content:
- The preserved record on Bobcat Movers is thinner than for some other clients in this workspace. Alex should treat several operational facts as **unverified** until checked live.
- Bobcat appears to be a brand where **positioning matters**: the site should not feel cheap, spammy, or bloated.
- The safest working assumption is that this account benefits from:
  - simple conversion paths
  - premium visual presentation
  - careful wording around quality/care
  - minimal fluff
- Before any new buildout or SEO push, Alex should do a quick live audit of:
  - site status
  - GBP status
  - current homepage messaging
  - access credentials / ownership
  - existing backlinks or recognition mentions already placed
